channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: American democracy requires that we ’be architects, not arsonists’
item.description: Eboo Patel believes learning to value and tolerate diversity is the engine that drives just and equitable societies. Can America learn to do it before the social fabric is irrevocably torn? That’s the discussion on this week’s Big Books and Bold Ideas, part of our Americans and Democracy series.
item.dc:creator: Kerri Miller and Kelly Gordon, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska high court restores voting right for thousands of convicted felons
item.description: The court's unanimous decision restores the right to vote for residents with felony criminal records upon completion of their sentence, impacting thousands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: VP Harris' 'Agenda for Black men' not as exclusive as advertised
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris' presidential campaign says that her proposed "Opportunity Agenda for Black Men" will be open to individuals of all races and genders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump rally shooting review finds 'deep flaws' in Secret Service
item.description: An independent review of the July 13 assassination attempt of former President Trump offered a critical review of the Secret Service, said the episode “reveal[s] deep flaws” in the agency and called for new leadership for the nation’s protective service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: FREDERICK, Md. (AP) — In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panel looking into Trump assassination attempt says Secret Service needs ‘fundamental reform’
item.description: An independent panel investigating the attempted assassination of Donald Trump at a Pennsylvania campaign rally says the Secret Services needs fundamental reform” and that “another Butler can and will happen again” without major changes in how candidates are protected.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump was asked to name three nice things about Harris. His response sounded like hers
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Donald Trump on Wednesday was asked to name three virtues of his Democratic opponent Kamala Harris, a week after Harris was asked to do the same for the Republican nominee. His answer sounded a lot like hers.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Robert Robertson’s execution hours away, supporters hope desperate measures will spare him
item.description: A Texas House panel issued an unprecedented subpoena for the death row inmate in last-ditch effort to halt his execution in alleged shaken baby case.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Harris is pressed on the concerns of Black voters in Charlamagne tha God town hall
item.description: Harris’ town hall with the influential radio host came at a crucial moment in the campaign, as polls show her struggling to cement the level of support she may need from Black men to win the White House.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hennepin County achieves 'functional zero' status in ending veteran homelessness
item.description: The designation from several federal agencies indicates a community has brought down the number of veterans experiencing homelessness to a very low level and continues to sustain the effort.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In divided St. Cloud, challengers hope to flip House seats
item.description: While many legislative districts in Minnesota are either solidly Republican or DFL, in the St. Cloud, voters are evenly split. Races for the two House seats that represent the region are among the most competitive in the state.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Migrant deaths in New Mexico have increased tenfold
item.description: Experts say smugglers are treating migrants more harshly and bringing them on paths that could be more dangerous in extreme summer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump rally arrest: Vem Miller says it's 'false allegations'
item.description: A self-proclaimed "kindergarten kid when it comes to gun," Miller claims the charges against him are all "false.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Who could replace interim NYPD Commissioner Donlon if he resigns?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to resign following a string of high-profile exits from City Hall over the last month, sources told PIX11 News on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Shondra Summers-Armstrong discusses key issues in Las Vegas city council race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) — Shondra Summers-Armstrong, currently serving as an Assemblywoman for District 6, is running for Las Vegas City Council, Ward 5.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
